meta.title: Jehi nomogram — chance of seizure freedom after epilepsy surgery
meta.authors: Jehi L, Yardi R, Chagin K, et al.
meta.outcome: Predicted chance of COMPLETE freedom from seizure recurrence at 2 and 5 years after resective epilepsy surgery, from six routinely available presurgical characteristics
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. The Jehi nomogram (Jehi et al., Lancet Neurology 2015) gives an individualised estimate of the chance of complete freedom from seizure recurrence at 2 and 5 years after resective epilepsy surgery, from six easily obtained presurgical characteristics. It was developed on 846 patients at the Cleveland Clinic and externally validated on 604 patients (concordance ~0.60).

How to read it

  • The two figures are the percent chance of being completely seizure-free (Engel-equivalent, isolated auras not counted) at 2 and 5 years.
  • Longer time from seizure onset to surgery worsens the outlook for frontal and posterior-quadrant resections but has little effect for temporal resections; a history of generalised tonic–clonic seizures, higher preoperative seizure frequency, and temporal or posterior (vs frontal) surgery lower the chance.
  • The waterfall shows how each factor moves this patient's estimate up or down from a baseline patient.

Demonstration tool reproducing the published nomogram — an aid to presurgical counselling, not a substitute for the epilepsy surgery conference.

predictors.name: Sex
predictors.assess: Patient sex. Male sex slightly lowers the chance of seizure freedom in the model (women = reference).

predictors.name: Type of surgery
predictors.assess: Resection type. Frontal is the reference; temporal and posterior-quadrant resections carry a higher baseline recurrence hazard. This choice also sets how strongly epilepsy duration matters (see below).

predictors.name: Time from seizure onset to surgery
predictors.assess: Years between age at first seizure and age at surgery (epilepsy duration). Longer duration reduces the chance of seizure freedom for frontal and posterior-quadrant resections, but has essentially no effect for temporal resections (an interaction built into the model).

predictors.name: Generalised tonic–clonic seizures
predictors.assess: History of secondarily generalised (focal-to-bilateral) tonic–clonic seizures. Their presence lowers the chance of seizure freedom.

predictors.name: Cause of seizures
predictors.assess: Pathological cause (from histology or MRI). MCD is the reference; tumour, MTS and stroke are somewhat more favourable, an 'other/unknown' cause less favourable.

predictors.name: Preoperative seizures per month
predictors.assess: Average monthly seizure frequency before surgery. Higher frequency lowers the chance of seizure freedom, with a diminishing (spline-shaped) effect at high counts. Range shown 0–100/month covers essentially all patients.

model.note: Predicts COMPLETE freedom from seizure recurrence (isolated auras and single early-postoperative or provoked seizures not counted). Baseline chance at the model mean is 57% (2 yr) / 40% (5 yr). Reproduces the published worked example (58% / 41%).
model.viz_info: Each characteristic adds or subtracts a weight (the published Cox coefficient) to a linear predictor, which is turned into the chance of remaining seizure-free at 2 and 5 years. The waterfall starts from a baseline patient and shows how each factor raises (green) or lowers (red) this patient's chance of seizure freedom.

provenance.source: Jehi 2015 Lancet Neurol Table 2 (Cox coefficients) + Figure 3 nomogram (seizures/month spline axis, scale calibration) + Figure 5 worked example — doi 10.1016/S1474-4422(14)70325-4
provenance.notes: Cox model, complete-seizure-freedom nomogram (Fig 3). Linear predictor from Table 2 coefficients: sex Men +0.1715; surgery Frontal 0/Posterior +0.3057/Temporal +0.6972; time-to-surgery slope by surgery type Frontal +0.0306, Posterior +0.0181 (=0.0306-0.0125), Temporal -0.0039 (=0.0306-0.0345) per year; GTCS +0.2493; cause MCD 0/MTS -0.1850/Tumour -0.1937/Stroke -0.1175/Other +0.2294; seizures/month via restricted-cubic-spline axis digitised from Fig 3 (pts/LP=50). Chance of freedom = S0^exp(LP-C), S0(2yr)=0.57, S0(5yr)=0.40, C=1.00 (centering fixed to the Fig 5 worked example). Nomogram scale confirmed: categorical point-spans match coefficients to <2px; freq near-0 slope 0.0141 = coef 0.0143. Digitised & built 2026-07-08.
